devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । ध्रुवं निवृत्तं प्रतिबुद्ध्य वैशसाद् । अपेतमन्युं भगवान् धनेश्वरः । तत्रागतश्चारणयक्षकिन्नरैः । संस्तूयमानो न्यवदत् कृताञ...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācadhruvaṃ nivṛttaṃ pratibuddhya vaiśasādapetamanyuṃ bhagavān dhaneśvaraḥtatrāgataścāraṇayakṣakinnaraiḥsaṃstūyamāno nyavadat kṛtāñjalim
translation:
Maitreya said:
Learning that Dhruva’s anger had been appeased and that he had desisted from slaughter, the divine Lord of Wealth (Kubera) who was being eulogised by Gāraṇas, Yakṣas and Kinnaras, arrived there, and spoke to him (Dhruva) who folded his hands (to pay respects to Kubera).

devanagari: धनद उवाच । भो भोः क्षत्रियदायाद परितुष्टोऽस्मि तेऽनघ । यत्त्वं पितामहादेशाद् वैरं दुस्त्यजमत्यजः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanada uvācabho bhoḥ kṣatriyadāyāda parituṣṭo'smi te'naghayattvaṃ pitāmahādeśād vairaṃ dustyajamatyajaḥ
translation:
Kubera (the donor of riches) said:
Oh sinless Kṣatriya Prince! I am extremely pleased with you, as at the behest of your grand-father, you have given up the feeling of enmity which is very difficult to renounce.

devanagari: न भवान् अवधीद्यक्षान् न यक्षा भ्रातरं तव । काल एव हि भूतानां प्रभुरप्ययभावयोः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: na bhavān avadhīdyakṣān na yakṣā bhrātaraṃ tavakāla eva hi bhūtānāṃ prabhurapyayabhāvayoḥ
translation: (The fact of the matter is that) neither you have killed the Yakṣas nor did they slay your brother. It is none but Kāla (the power of God called Time), which is responsible for the creation and destruction of created beings.

devanagari: अहं त्वमित्यपार्था धीः अज्ञानात् पुरुषस्य हि । स्वाप्नीवाभात्यतद्ध्यानाद् यया बन्धविपर्ययौ ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ tvamityapārthā dhīḥ ajñānāt puruṣasya hisvāpnīvābhātyataddhyānād yayā bandhaviparyayau
translation: Just as a notion in the state of dreaming is false and meaningless, so is the notion of difference as ‘I’ and ‘You’ which arises in man due to his ignorance, and on account of continuous thinking of the physical body as the Soul. Bondage of saṃsāra and miseries are due to this notion.

devanagari: तद्‍गच्छ ध्रुव भद्रं ते भगवन्तं अधोक्षजम् । सर्वभूतात्मभावेन सर्वभूतात्मविग्रहम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tad‍gaccha dhruva bhadraṃ te bhagavantaṃ adhokṣajamsarvabhūtātmabhāvena sarvabhūtātmavigraham
translation: Oh Dhruva! Please return now. God bless you. For the sake of Liberation from Saṃsāra, adore Lord Adhokṣaja (Viṣṇu) who manifests himself in the bodies of all creatures, and consider that he is the antaryāmin (indwelling Soul) abiding in all beings[1].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Or: considering all beings as your own self.

devanagari: भजस्व भजनीयाङ्घ्रिं अभवाय भवच्छिदम् । युक्तं विरहितं शक्त्या गुणमय्यात्ममायया ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhajasva bhajanīyāṅghriṃ abhavāya bhavacchidamyuktaṃ virahitaṃ śaktyā guṇamayyātmamāyayā
translation: For terminating Saṃsāra (the cycle of births and deaths), please worship him whose feet are worth resorting, and who destroys saṃsāra, and who, though associated with his own māyāśakti constituted of the three Guṇas (attributes—Sattva, Rajas and Tamas), is yet aloof from it.

devanagari: वृणीहि कामं नृप यन्मनोगतं । मत्तस्त्वमौत्तानपदेऽविशङ्कितः । वरं वरार्होऽम्बुजनाभपादयोः । अनन्तरं त्वां वयमङ्ग शुश्रुम ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛṇīhi kāmaṃ nṛpa yanmanogataṃmattastvamauttānapade'viśaṅkitaḥvaraṃ varārho'mbujanābhapādayoḥanantaraṃ tvāṃ vayamaṅga śuśruma
translation: Oh son of Uttānapāda, we have learnt that you are very close to Viṣṇu’s (God with a lotus in His navel) feet. Oh King Dhruva, as you deserve to receive a boon, ask of me freely and without any hesitation, any boon at will, cherished by you in your heart.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । स राजराजेन वराय चोदितो । ध्रुवो महाभागवतो महामतिः । हरौ स वव्रेऽचलितां स्मृतिं यया । तरत्ययत्‍नेन दुरत्ययं तमः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācasa rājarājena varāya coditodhruvo mahābhāgavato mahāmatiḥharau sa vavre'calitāṃ smṛtiṃ yayātaratyayat‍nena duratyayaṃ tamaḥ
translation:
Maitreya said:
The great devotee of the Lord, that highly intelligent Dhruva, who was thus induced by Kubera (the king of Kings) to ask for a boon (from him), sought to be blest with the constant remembrance of Hari, whereby one effortlessly goes beyond the darkness (of ignorance or saṃsāra) which is very difficult to cross.

devanagari: तस्य प्रीतेन मनसा तां दत्त्वैडविडस्ततः । पश्यतोऽन्तर्दधे सोऽपि स्वपुरं प्रत्यपद्यत ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya prītena manasā tāṃ dattvaiḍaviḍastataḥpaśyato'ntardadhe so'pi svapuraṃ pratyapadyata
translation: With a glad heart, Kubera (the son of Iḍaviḍā), having conferred that boon (on Dhruva), immediately disappeared while Dhruva was gazing. He also repaired to his capital.

devanagari: अथायजत यज्ञेशं क्रतुभिर्भूरिदक्षिणैः । द्रव्यक्रियादेवतानां कर्म कर्मफलप्रदम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: athāyajata yajñeśaṃ kratubhirbhūridakṣiṇaiḥdravyakriyādevatānāṃ karma karmaphalapradam
translation: Thereafter, by performing sacrifices with liberal donations and sacrificial fees, he worshipped the Lord of Sacrifices who is both the donor of the fruit of religious karmas, and is himself the reward of the religious duties (sacrifices). He performed them with proper materials as per ritualistic technique and with due offerings to Gods.

devanagari: सर्वात्मनि अच्युतेऽसर्वे तीव्रौघां भक्तिमुद्वहन् । ददर्शात्मनि भूतेषु तं एव अवस्थितं विभुम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvātmani acyute'sarve tīvraughāṃ bhaktimudvahandadarśātmani bhūteṣu taṃ eva avasthitaṃ vibhum
translation: Bearing an intense flow of devotion to Acyuta (Viṣṇu) who abides in all as their Soul, yet is distinct from them, he visualised the All-pervading Lord as dwelling within himself and in all other beings.

devanagari: तमेवं शीलसम्पन्नं ब्रह्मण्यं दीनवत्सलम् । गोप्तारं धर्मसेतूनां मेनिरे पितरं प्रजाः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tamevaṃ śīlasampannaṃ brahmaṇyaṃ dīnavatsalamgoptāraṃ dharmasetūnāṃ menire pitaraṃ prajāḥ
translation: The subjects considered him as their father (protector)—him, who was of such noble character, friendly to Brāhmaṇas, compassionate to the afflicted, and the protector of the bounds of religion.

devanagari: षट्त्रिंशद् वर्षसाहस्रं शशास क्षितिमण्डलम् । भोगैः पुण्यक्षयं कुर्वन् अभोगैः अशुभक्षयम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: ṣaṭtriṃśad varṣasāhasraṃ śaśāsa kṣitimaṇḍalambhogaiḥ puṇyakṣayaṃ kurvan abhogaiḥ aśubhakṣayam
translation: Exhausting the fund of his meritorious deeds by enjoyments of pleasures, and the balance of inauspicious deeds by performing sacrifices and other religious acts, he governed the sphere of the earth for thirty-six thousand years.

devanagari: एवं बहुसवं कालं महात्माविचलेन्द्रियः । त्रिवर्गौपयिकं नीत्वा पुत्रायादान् नृपासनम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ bahusavaṃ kālaṃ mahātmāvicalendriyaḥtrivargaupayikaṃ nītvā putrāyādān nṛpāsanam
translation: In this way, the great-Souled king of perfectly controlled senses, passed a long time in performing a number of sacrifices which result in the attainment of the Trinity—of Dharma, Ārtha and Kāma. He then regarded this universe as being illusory—constructed by Māyā on the basis of the Soul, like the dream city of Gandharvas created by Avidyā (Māyā or Nescience), and abdicated the throne in favour of his son.

devanagari: मन्यमान इदं विश्वं मायारचितमात्मनि । अविद्यारचितस्वप्न गन्धर्वनगरोपमम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: manyamāna idaṃ viśvaṃ māyāracitamātmaniavidyāracitasvapna gandharvanagaropamam
translation: In this way, the great-Souled king of perfectly controlled senses, passed a long time in performing a number of sacrifices which result in the attainment of the Trinity—of Dharma, Ārtha and Kāma. He then regarded this universe as being illusory—constructed by Māyā on the basis of the Soul, like the dream city of Gandharvas created by Avidyā (Māyā or Nescience), and abdicated the throne in favour of his son.

devanagari: आत्मस्त्र्यपत्यसुहृदो बलमृद्धकोशम् । अन्तःपुरं परिविहारभुवश्च रम्याः । भूमण्डलं जलधिमेखलमाकलय्य । कालोपसृष्टमिति स प्रययौ विशालाम् ॥ १६...
english_devnagari: ātmastryapatyasuhṛdo balamṛddhakośamantaḥpuraṃ parivihārabhuvaśca ramyāḥbhūmaṇḍalaṃ jaladhimekhalamākalayyakālopasṛṣṭamiti sa prayayau viśālām
translation: Comprehending that his own body, wife, children, friends, army, rich treasury, harem, beautiful places of sport and recreations and the whole sphere of the earth engirdled by the ocean, are subject to destruction and hence transitory, he proceeded to Viśālā (Badarikāśrama).

devanagari: तस्यां विशुद्धकरणः शिववार्विगाह्य । बद्ध्वाऽसनं जितमरुन्मनसाऽहृताक्षः । स्थूले दधार भगवत्प्रतिरूप एतद् । ध्यायन् तदव्यवहितो व्यसृजत्समा...
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ viśuddhakaraṇaḥ śivavārvigāhyabaddhvā'sanaṃ jitamarunmanasā'hṛtākṣaḥsthūle dadhāra bhagavatpratirūpa etaddhyāyan tadavyavahito vyasṛjatsamādhau
translation: Having performed ablutions in the sacred waters thereof, he purified his mind (by observing yama and niyama, the code of Yogic discipline). Having steadied himself firmly in a yogic posture, and controlled his breath (through prāṇāyāma), and restraining his senses by his mind, he concentrated his mind on the gross (Cosmic) form of the Lord, viz. the Virāṭ-rūpa. In course of meditation, the consciousness of the distinction between the meditator and the object of meditation, was lost. Being absorbed in meditation, he forgot the thought of that gross (Cosmic) form also.

devanagari: भक्तिं हरौ भगवति प्रवहन्नजस्रम् । आनन्दबाष्पकलया मुहुरर्द्यमानः । विक्लिद्यमानहृदयः पुलकाचिताङ्गो । नात्मानमस्मरदसाविति मुक्तलिङ्गः ॥ १...
english_devnagari: bhaktiṃ harau bhagavati pravahannajasramānandabāṣpakalayā muhurardyamānaḥviklidyamānahṛdayaḥ pulakācitāṅgonātmānamasmaradasāviti muktaliṅgaḥ
translation: He cherished constant devotion to the venerable Lord Hari. He was now and then overwhelmed with tears of joy. His heart melted (with emotions for the Lord), and his hair stood on their ends all over the body. He detached himself from his gross body, and did not remember that he was so-and-so (Dhruva).

devanagari: स ददर्श विमानाग्र्यं नभसोऽवतरद् ध्रुवः । विभ्राजयद् दश दिशो राकापतिमिवोदितम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa dadarśa vimānāgryaṃ nabhaso'vatarad dhruvaḥvibhrājayad daśa diśo rākāpatimivoditam
translation: Dhruva noticed an excellent heavenly car descending from the heavens, illumining the ten directions like the rising full moon.

devanagari: तत्रानु देवप्रवरौ चतुर्भुजौ । श्यामौ किशोरावरुणाम्बुजेक्षणौ । स्थिताववष्टभ्य गदां सुवाससौ । किरीटहाराङ्गदचारुकुण्डलौ ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tatrānu devapravarau caturbhujauśyāmau kiśorāvaruṇāmbujekṣaṇausthitāvavaṣṭabhya gadāṃ suvāsasaukirīṭahārāṅgadacārukuṇḍalau
translation: Later on, in that heavenly car, (he saw) two prominent gods in the prime of their youth. They were four-armed of dark complexion and with eyes (beautiful) like red lotuses. They were richly attired, and were adorned with crowns, pearl necklaces, armlets and beautiful ear-rings. They stood leaning against their maces.

devanagari: विज्ञाय तावुत्तमगायकिङ्करौ । अभ्युत्थितः साध्वसविस्मृतक्रमः । ननाम नामानि गृणन्मधुद्विषः । पार्षत्प्रधानौ इति संहताञ्जलिः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: vijñāya tāvuttamagāyakiṅkarauabhyutthitaḥ sādhvasavismṛtakramaḥnanāma nāmāni gṛṇanmadhudviṣaḥpārṣatpradhānau iti saṃhatāñjaliḥ
translation: Comprehending them to be the attendants of Hari (the God of excellent renown), he stood up (to show respect). Forgetting the due procedure of worship in the hurry of that (agitated) moment, he folded his hands uttering the names of Hari, as he thought them to be the chief attendants of Viṣṇu (the slayer of the demon Madhu).

devanagari: तं कृष्णपादाभिनिविष्टचेतसं । बद्धाञ्जलिं प्रश्रयनम्रकन्धरम् । सुनन्दनन्दावुपसृत्य सस्मितं । प्रत्यूचतुः पुष्करनाभसम्मतौ ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ kṛṣṇapādābhiniviṣṭacetasaṃbaddhāñjaliṃ praśrayanamrakandharamsunandanandāvupasṛtya sasmitaṃpratyūcatuḥ puṣkaranābhasammatau
translation: Sunanda and Nanda, the esteemed servants of Viṣṇu (the lotus-navelled god) approached him (Dhruva) who had concentrated his mind on Kṛṣṇa’s (Viṣṇu’s) feet; had folded his palms and bowed down his neck in humility.

devanagari: सुनन्दनन्दावूचतुः । भो भो राजन् सुभद्रं ते वाचं नोऽवहितः श्रृणु । यः पञ्चवर्षस्तपसा भवान् देवमतीतृपत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: sunandanandāvūcatuḥbho bho rājan subhadraṃ te vācaṃ no'vahitaḥ śrṛṇuyaḥ pañcavarṣastapasā bhavān devamatītṛpat
translation:
Sunanda and Nanda said:
Oh King! May you be extremely blessed! Your honour, who, while yet a child of five, had pleased the Lord by your penance, listen to our speech attentively.

devanagari: तस्याखिलजगद्धातुः आवां देवस्य शार्ङ्गिणः । पार्षदौ इह सम्प्राप्तौ नेतुं त्वां भगवत्पदम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyākhilajagaddhātuḥ āvāṃ devasya śārṅgiṇaḥpārṣadau iha samprāptau netuṃ tvāṃ bhagavatpadam
translation: We both are the attendants of the God with the Śārṅga bow (Viṣṇu) who is the sustainer of the whole world. Both of us have arrived here for taking you to the Lord’s abode.

devanagari: सुदुर्जयं विष्णुपदं जितं त्वया । यत्सूरयोऽप्राप्य विचक्षते परम् । आतिष्ठ तच्चन्द्रदिवाकरादयो । ग्रहर्क्षताराः परियन्ति दक्षिणम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sudurjayaṃ viṣṇupadaṃ jitaṃ tvayāyatsūrayo'prāpya vicakṣate paramātiṣṭha taccandradivākarādayograharkṣatārāḥ pariyanti dakṣiṇam
translation: You have successfully attained to the region of Viṣṇu which is extremely difficult to secure, and to which sages, (the saptarṣis—Ursa Major) not being able to reach, merely look up from below. Now occupy that place round which (luminous bodies like) the Moon, the Sun (and other luminaries), planets, constellations of stars and (independent) stars go, keeping it to their right.

devanagari: अनास्थितं ते पितृभिः अन्यैरप्यङ्ग कर्हिचित् । आतिष्ठ जगतां वन्द्यं तद्विष्णोः परमं पदम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: anāsthitaṃ te pitṛbhiḥ anyairapyaṅga karhicitātiṣṭha jagatāṃ vandyaṃ tadviṣṇoḥ paramaṃ padam
translation: Dear (Dhruva), take your abode in the realm which has never been occupied by your forefathers. That highest region of Viṣṇu is adorable to all worlds.

devanagari: एतद्विमानप्रवरं उत्तमश्लोकमौलिना । उपस्थापितमायुष्मन् अधिरोढुं त्वमर्हसि ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: etadvimānapravaraṃ uttamaślokamaulināupasthāpitamāyuṣman adhiroḍhuṃ tvamarhasi
translation: Oh long-lived one, you should now ascend this excellent aerial car, despatched to you by Viṣṇu who is the foremost among persons of meritorious renown.[2]

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । निशम्य वैकुण्ठनियोज्यमुख्ययोः । मधुच्युतं वाचमुरुक्रमप्रियः । कृताभिषेकः कृतनित्यमङ्गलो । मुनीन् प्रणम्याशिषमभ्यवादयत् ॥...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaniśamya vaikuṇṭhaniyojyamukhyayoḥmadhucyutaṃ vācamurukramapriyaḥkṛtābhiṣekaḥ kṛtanityamaṅgalomunīn praṇamyāśiṣamabhyavādayat
translation:
Maitreya said:
Having heard the extremely sweet (honey-dripping) words of the two prominent deputies of Lord Viṣṇu, Dhruva, the beloved devotee of Viṣṇu[3], took his bath, performed his daily auspicious duties, paid his obeisance to sages and received their blessings.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Urukrama: Viṣṇu, so called because in the Vāmana incarnation he took very long strides to occupy the three worlds from Bali.

devanagari: परीत्याभ्यर्च्य धिष्ण्याग्र्यं पार्षदौ अवभिवन्द्य च । इयेष तदधिष्ठातुं बिभ्रद्रूपं हिरण्मयम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: parītyābhyarcya dhiṣṇyāgryaṃ pārṣadau avabhivandya caiyeṣa tadadhiṣṭhātuṃ bibhradrūpaṃ hiraṇmayam
translation: Having circumambulated that excellent heavenly car, he worshipped it. He bowed down to both the attendants of Viṣṇu. Assuming a form brilliant like gold, he desired to get into the car.

devanagari: तदोत्तानपदः पुत्रो ददर्शान्तकमागतम् । मृत्योर्मूर्ध्नि पदं दत्त्वा आरुरोहाद्भुतं गृहम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tadottānapadaḥ putro dadarśāntakamāgatammṛtyormūrdhni padaṃ dattvā ārurohādbhutaṃ gṛham
translation: At that time, when the son of Uttānapāda (Dhruva) saw that the god of Death had come, he placed his foot on the head of that god (of Death)[4] and mounted that wonderful aerial mansion.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā narrates the episode thus: When Dhruva was about to enter that heavenly car, the God of Death arrived there, bowed to him and requested him to accept him. Dhruva welcomed him, requested him to sit down for a moment; and remembering Lord Viṣṇu placed his foot on the head of that God (Death) and ascended into the heavenly car.

devanagari: तदा दुन्दुभयो नेदुः मृदङ्गपणवादयः । गन्धर्वमुख्याः प्रजगुः पेतुः कुसुमवृष्टयः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā dundubhayo neduḥ mṛdaṅgapaṇavādayaḥgandharvamukhyāḥ prajaguḥ petuḥ kusumavṛṣṭayaḥ
translation: At the very moment, drums and musical instruments like Paṇava, etc. were sounded. Prominent Gandharvas sang his praise. Showers of flowers fell on him from above.

devanagari: स च स्वर्लोकमारोक्ष्यन् सुनीतिं जननीं ध्रुवः । अन्वस्मरदगं हित्वा दीनां यास्ये त्रिविष्टपम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ca svarlokamārokṣyan sunītiṃ jananīṃ dhruvaḥanvasmaradagaṃ hitvā dīnāṃ yāsye triviṣṭapam
translation: As he was about to fly to the heavenly regions, Dhruva suddenly remembered his mother Sunīti, (saying to himself), ‘How am I to proceed to the inaccessible celestial region, leaving my poor mother behind?’

devanagari: इति व्यवसितं तस्य व्यवसाय सुरोत्तमौ । दर्शयामासतुर्देवीं पुरो यानेन गच्छतीम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti vyavasitaṃ tasya vyavasāya surottamaudarśayāmāsaturdevīṃ puro yānena gacchatīm
translation: Having understood this wish of Dhruva, the two excellent gods showed to him the brilliant queen-mother Sunīti—going ahead of him, in an aerial car.

devanagari: तत्र तत्र प्रशंसद्भिः पथि वैमानिकैः सुरैः । अवकीर्यमाणो ददृशे कुसुमैः क्रमशो ग्रहान् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra tatra praśaṃsadbhiḥ pathi vaimānikaiḥ suraiḥavakīryamāṇo dadṛśe kusumaiḥ kramaśo grahān
translation: At every stage on way, Dhruva was being showered with flowers and praised by gods seated in their heavenly cars; and he went past planets, one after another.

devanagari: त्रिलोकीं देवयानेन सोऽतिव्रज्य मुनीनपि । परस्ताद्यद्ध्रुवगतिः विष्णोः पदमथाभ्यगात् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: trilokīṃ devayānena so'tivrajya munīnapiparastādyaddhruvagatiḥ viṣṇoḥ padamathābhyagāt
translation: Then he (Dhruva) who was blest with an eternal position, went by the devayāna path (or by the celestial car sent to him by the Lord), beyond the three worlds and the astral positions of the seven sages (Ursa Major), and reached the abode of Viṣṇu which lies beyond them.

devanagari: यद्भ्राजमानं स्वरुचैव सर्वतो । लोकास्त्रयो ह्यनु विभ्राजन्त एते । यन्नाव्रजन्जन्तुषु येऽननुग्रहा । व्रजन्ति भद्राणि चरन्ति येऽनिशम् ॥ ३...
english_devnagari: yadbhrājamānaṃ svarucaiva sarvatolokāstrayo hyanu vibhrājanta eteyannāvrajanjantuṣu ye'nanugrahāvrajanti bhadrāṇi caranti ye'niśam
translation: It (Viṣṇu’s abode) shines brilliant by its own splendour. It illuminates these three worlds (the earth, the heaven etc.) by its light. The people who are not compassionate unto living beings never attain to this realm. Only those who always perform auspicious deeds, reach there.

devanagari: शान्ताः समदृशः शुद्धाः सर्वभूतानुरञ्जनाः । यान्त्यञ्जसाच्युतपदं अच्युतप्रियबान्धवाः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: śāntāḥ samadṛśaḥ śuddhāḥ sarvabhūtānurañjanāḥyāntyañjasācyutapadaṃ acyutapriyabāndhavāḥ
translation: Those who are of tranquil mind, who look upon all beings as equals, whose minds are cleansed of all sins and impurities (like rajas and tamas), who are pleasing to all beings, who look upon Acyuta (Viṣṇu) as their dear relative—those easily reach the abode of Viṣṇu.

devanagari: इत्युत्तानपदः पुत्रो ध्रुवः कृष्णपरायणः । अभूत्त्रयाणां लोकानां चूडामणिरिवामलः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuttānapadaḥ putro dhruvaḥ kṛṣṇaparāyaṇaḥabhūttrayāṇāṃ lokānāṃ cūḍāmaṇirivāmalaḥ
translation: In this way, Dhruva, the son of Uttānapāda who was solely devoted to Kṛṣṇa (Viṣṇu), became as if a refulgent crest-jewel of the three worlds.

devanagari: गम्भीरवेगोऽनिमिषं ज्योतिषां चक्रमाहितम् । यस्मिन् भ्रमति कौरव्य मेढ्यामिव गवां गणः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: gambhīravego'nimiṣaṃ jyotiṣāṃ cakramāhitamyasmin bhramati kauravya meḍhyāmiva gavāṃ gaṇaḥ
translation: Oh Vidura (descendant of Kuru)! Riveted to this (Dhruva’s) post, the whole group of heavenly luminaries revolve round it ceaselessly, just as (pairs of) bullocks tied to a post in the middle of the threshing ground, go round with great speed (for threshing out the corn by trampling).

devanagari: महिमानं विलोक्यास्य नारदो भगवान् ऋषिः । आतोद्यं वितुदन् श्लोकान् सत्रेऽगायत् प्रचेतसाम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: mahimānaṃ vilokyāsya nārado bhagavān ṛṣiḥātodyaṃ vitudan ślokān satre'gāyat pracetasām
translation: Having seen the great glory of Dhruva, the venerable sage Nārada, playing on his lute, sang the (following) verses at the sacrificial session of Pracetas:

devanagari: नारद उवाच । नूनं सुनीतेः पतिदेवतायाः । तपःप्रभावस्य सुतस्य तां गतिम् । दृष्ट्वाभ्युपायानपि वेदवादिनो । नैवाधिगन्तुं प्रभवन्ति किं नृपाः...
english_devnagari: nārada uvācanūnaṃ sunīteḥ patidevatāyāḥtapaḥprabhāvasya sutasya tāṃ gatimdṛṣṭvābhyupāyānapi vedavādinonaivādhigantuṃ prabhavanti kiṃ nṛpāḥ
translation:
Nārada said:
Despite their knowledge of the means, Brāhmaṇa sages, the expounders of Vedas, are not able to reach the attainment secured by the dint of his austere penance, by Dhruva, the son of Sunīti who looked upon her husband as god. How can then, kings attain to it?

devanagari: यः पञ्चवर्षो गुरुदारवाक्शरैः । भिन्नेन यातो हृदयेन दूयता । वनं मदादेशकरोऽजितं प्रभुं । जिगाय तद्भक्तगुणैः पराजितम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ pañcavarṣo gurudāravākśaraiḥbhinnena yāto hṛdayena dūyatāvanaṃ madādeśakaro'jitaṃ prabhuṃjigāya tadbhaktaguṇaiḥ parājitam
translation: As a child of five, he went to the forest with his heart afflicted by being wounded with the arrow-like words of his stepmother. Abiding by my advice, he conquered i.e. won the favour of the unconquerable Lord who is overpowered only by the excellent qualities of his devotees.

devanagari: यः क्षत्रबन्धुर्भुवि तस्याधिरूढं । अन्वारुरुक्षेदपि वर्षपूगैः । षट्पञ्चवर्षो यदहोभिरल्पैः । प्रसाद्य वैकुण्ठमवाप तत्पदम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ kṣatrabandhurbhuvi tasyādhirūḍhaṃanvārurukṣedapi varṣapūgaiḥṣaṭpañcavarṣo yadahobhiralpaiḥprasādya vaikuṇṭhamavāpa tatpadam
translation: Can an ordinary Kṣatriya on the earth even imagine to reach after many years to the exalted position occupied by Dhruva who, as a child of five or six years, secured it by propitiating Lord Viṣṇu within a few days?

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एतत्तेऽभिहितं सर्वं यत्पृष्टोऽहमिह त्वया । ध्रुवस्योद्दामयशसः चरितं सम्मतं सताम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaetatte'bhihitaṃ sarvaṃ yatpṛṣṭo'hamiha tvayādhruvasyoddāmayaśasaḥ caritaṃ sammataṃ satām
translation:
Maitreya continued:
I have narrated to you everything that you asked me here about the life-story of Dhruva of excellent fame—the life story which is held in high regard by the righteous.

devanagari: धन्यं यशस्यमायुष्यं पुण्यं स्वस्त्ययनं महत् । स्वर्ग्यं ध्रौव्यं सौमनस्यं प्रशस्यमघमर्षणम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanyaṃ yaśasyamāyuṣyaṃ puṇyaṃ svastyayanaṃ mahatsvargyaṃ dhrauvyaṃ saumanasyaṃ praśasyamaghamarṣaṇam
translation: If heard and recited, it (Dhruva’s life story) leads to wealth, glory and longevity; it is meritorious and brings in great auspiciousness; it helps to attain to the celestial region and to reach even the eternal abode; it leads to godhood; it is extremely praiseworthy, and cleanses all sins.

devanagari: श्रुत्वैतत् श्रद्धयाभीक्ष्णं अच्युतप्रियचेष्टितम् । भवेद्भक्तिर्भगवति यया स्यात् क्लेशसङ्क्षयः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvaitat śraddhayābhīkṣṇaṃ acyutapriyaceṣṭitambhavedbhaktirbhagavati yayā syāt kleśasaṅkṣayaḥ
translation: By constantly listening with faith and devotion the lifestory of Dhruva who was dear to Lord Acyuta (Viṣṇu), devotion unto the Lord will be engendered, which will lead to the destruction of all miseries.

devanagari: महत्त्वमिच्छतां तीर्थं श्रोतुः शीलादयो गुणाः । यत्र तेजस्तदिच्छूनां मानो यत्र मनस्विनाम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: mahattvamicchatāṃ tīrthaṃ śrotuḥ śīlādayo guṇāḥyatra tejastadicchūnāṃ māno yatra manasvinām
translation: Here this story is a sacred fountain of greatness for those who seek it. When it is heard, virtues like good-naturedness generate in the listeners. Those who desire bravery and splendour get it developed (in them by listening to it). It bestows respectability on the noble-minded (listeners who aspire after it).

devanagari: प्रयतः कीर्तयेत्प्रातः समवाये द्विजन्मनाम् । सायं च पुण्यश्लोकस्य ध्रुवस्य चरितं महत् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: prayataḥ kīrtayetprātaḥ samavāye dvijanmanāmsāyaṃ ca puṇyaślokasya dhruvasya caritaṃ mahat
translation: One should recite both in the morning and in the evening, the glorious life, with a concentrated attention, of Dhruva of sacred glory, in the assembly of the Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: पौर्णमास्यां सिनीवाल्यां द्वादश्यां श्रवणेऽथवा । दिनक्षये व्यतीपाते सङ्क्रमेऽर्कदिनेऽपि वा ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: paurṇamāsyāṃ sinīvālyāṃ dvādaśyāṃ śravaṇe'thavādinakṣaye vyatīpāte saṅkrame'rkadine'pi vā
translation: Or one who resorts to the sacred feet of Hari, should narrate to the faithful (audience, the life of Dhruva) on the full-moon day or the new moon day, the twelfth day of a fortnight or when the moon passes by the constellation of stars called Śravaṇa or on dina-kṣaya or on (the astronomically inauspicious yoga called) the Vyatipāta, or on the Saṅkramaṇa day (when the Sun crosses the sign of the zodiac) or on Sundays. He should cherish no desire and be self-contented; thereby he attains Liberation.

devanagari: श्रावयेत् श्रद्दधानानां तीर्थपादपदाश्रयः । नेच्छन् तत्रात्मनात्मानं सन्तुष्ट इति सिध्यति ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: śrāvayet śraddadhānānāṃ tīrthapādapadāśrayaḥnecchan tatrātmanātmānaṃ santuṣṭa iti sidhyati
translation: Or one who resorts to the sacred feet of Hari, should narrate to the faithful (audience, the life of Dhruva) on the full-moon day or the new moon day, the twelfth day of a fortnight or when the moon passes by the constellation of stars called Śravaṇa or on dina-kṣaya or on (the astronomically inauspicious yoga called) the Vyatipāta, or on the Saṅkramaṇa day (when the Sun crosses the sign of the zodiac) or on Sundays. He should cherish no desire and be self-contented; thereby he attains Liberation.

devanagari: ज्ञानमज्ञाततत्त्वाय यो दद्यात्सत्पथेऽमृतम् । कृपालोर्दीननाथस्य देवास्तस्यानुगृह्णते ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: jñānamajñātatattvāya yo dadyātsatpathe'mṛtamkṛpālordīnanāthasya devāstasyānugṛhṇate
translation: Gods bestow favours on the merciful man who is helpful to the afflicted and who imparts this knowledge (which is like) nectar in the pathway to God, to persons who are ignorant of the Truth.

devanagari: इदं मया तेऽभिहितं कुरूद्वह । ध्रुवस्य विख्यातविशुद्धकर्मणः । हित्वार्भकः क्रीडनकानि मातुः । गृहं च विष्णुं शरणं यो जगाम ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: idaṃ mayā te'bhihitaṃ kurūdvahadhruvasya vikhyātaviśuddhakarmaṇaḥhitvārbhakaḥ krīḍanakāni mātuḥgṛhaṃ ca viṣṇuṃ śaraṇaṃ yo jagāma
translation:
Oh Vidura (the foremost descendant of Kuru), I have narrated to you this biography of Dhruva who is well- known for pious deeds, and who, as a child, abandoned his toys and mother’s house, and resorted to Viṣṇu.
[back to top]
